Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
221	- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
222	- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
223	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
224	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
225	- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
226	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
227	  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
228	  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
229	  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
230	  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.
231	---
232	
233	## 2026-04-17 13:00 — US-008
234	- Applied `us_008_notifications_events` via Supabase MCP: `public.notifications` (6 cols, inbox + unread partial indexes), `public.push_tokens` (6 cols, unique `expo_token`, ios/android check), `public.events` (bigserial PK audit log with composite `(entity, entity_id, created_at desc)` index). RLS enabled on all three; policies deferred to US-009. `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` enforces app-role append-only per spec §4.4 line 1086.
235	- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: three new tables appear with correct shapes (events.id → `number` from bigserial; notifications.kind → `notification_kind` enum; push_tokens.platform → `string` since the spec uses a CHECK not an enum).
236	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417130000_us_008_notifications_events.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
237	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables + the 12 pre-existing — all deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
238	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One medium observation flagged: append-only enforcement scope for `events` — migration matches the spec literally but service_role retains UPDATE/DELETE grants. Promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-016+ handoff (edge functions writing to events must discipline themselves to INSERT-only; no DB trigger backstop exists).
239	- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.
240	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
241	  - Tables that the spec defines for future-version features (e.g. push_tokens for v1.1) should still be created when they appear in the same §4.7 block as an in-scope table. Partial block creation causes drift that later iterations would have to reconcile.
242	  - `revoke all on <table> from anon, authenticated` + RLS-enabled + zero policies = full deny for app roles. Useful pattern for anything in `public` that must be service-role-only (events-style audit logs, internal job queues).
243	  - When the reviewer flags a concern that the spec itself codifies, surface it as a forward-looking Codebase Patterns handoff rather than a migration-time fix — fixing it in the story would drift from the spec, which has a stronger hard-rule in this repo.
244	---
245	
246	## 2026-04-17 18:20 — US-014
247	- Applied `us_014_auth_signup_lister_fn` via Supabase MCP: `public.auth_signup_lister(p_username, p_email, p_org_name, p_website_url)` — PL/pgSQL RPC returning jsonb `{user_id}` on success or `{error: USERNAME_TAKEN|EMAIL_TAKEN}`. Inserts users + lister_profiles in a single implicit transaction; `get stacked diagnostics constraint_name` maps the unique_violation to the matching 409 code. Revoked from anon/authenticated/public; granted to service_role only.
248	- Deployed edge function `auth-signup-lister` (v3 after one redeploy for reviewer fix) with `verify_jwt=false` — this is how a lister gets their first JWT. Validates body (non-empty `username/email/org_name`), calls the RPC with the service-role client, mints JWT via `_shared/jwt.ts` (`{sub, role: 'lister', session_id}`), returns `{token, user_id, role}`.
249	- Provisioned `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` on the project via `supabase secrets set --env-file` (CLI, not MCP — MCP has no secrets tool). Value never echoed.
250	- Smoke test `scripts/test/auth-signup-lister.ts` covers all four branches: 200 happy path, 409 USERNAME_TAKEN, 400 INVALID_REQUEST (missing org_name), 409 EMAIL_TAKEN (fresh username + recycled email). All four pass end-to-end against the deployed function.
251	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417180000_us_014_auth_signup_lister_fn.sql` (new), `supabase/functions/auth-signup-lister/index.ts` (new), `scripts/test/auth-signup-lister.ts` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — `auth_signup_lister` in Functions block), `.gitignore` (added `supabase/.temp/` — CLI local cache from `secrets set`).
252	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings, only pre-existing INFOs) / mobile-mcp n/a (edge-function-only story)
253	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Medium: (1) added inline comment in RPC exception block documenting that `users_username_key` / `users_email_key` are Postgres's defaults for the inline `unique` declarations in US-004 — so a future constraint rename doesn't silently downgrade 409s to 500s; (2) extended smoke test with a 4th case to actually exercise the EMAIL_TAKEN branch. Nit: simplified `websiteUrl ?? undefined` → `websiteUrl` in the RPC args).
254	- Spec gap: §5.1 requires per-IP rate limiting via a `signup_attempts` table — that table does not exist yet and is deferred (already noted in the §16 spec-gap pattern for jobs/tables not yet scheduled). Documented inline in both migration and edge function headers so future iterations can wire it up alongside the webhook/session stories.
255	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
256	  - Atomic multi-insert flow pattern (PL/pgSQL RPC + unique_violation constraint-name mapping) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
257	  - Secret-management workflow for edge functions (CLI `supabase secrets set --env-file`, never MCP) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
258	  - Smoke-test structure for edge functions (`scripts/test/<fn>.ts` with `bun run`, `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey` headers even when `verify_jwt=false`) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
259	  - Default constraint-naming convention (`<table>_<column>_key` for anonymous inline `unique`) promoted to Codebase Patterns — every exception-handler string is coupled to this convention.
260	  - Reviewer's first review flagged two correctness-fragility issues (constraint-name coupling, untested branch) that looked like nits but would have been real regressions if a downstream migration renamed the constraints — underscores why the mandatory review step matters even on "small" edge-function stories.
261	---
262

file.content:
# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.
- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).
- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).
- **Edge-function smoke tests live under `scripts/test/<fn-name>.ts`, invoked with `bun run`.** Read `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from `process.env` (bun auto-loads `.env`). Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.

- Applied `us_012_seed` via Supabase MCP: one DO block seeds fixed-UUID rows — `acme_studio` lister + `lister_profiles`, `seed_creator` creator + `creator_profiles` + tiktok/instagram social_links, one active listing "Promote Acme Headphones" with listing_version v1 (2 pre-conditions — `min_followers` tiktok/instagram, 2 post-conditions — `post_family_friendly` bool + `post_must_mention` contains, 1 sample_video), and three metric_snapshots (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts) that the US-007 BEFORE-INSERT trigger mirrors into creator_profiles.
- Verified via `execute_sql`: creator_profiles denorm columns all populated — tiktok_follower_count=42000, tiktok_avg_views_last_10=18500, tiktok_total_likes=850000, tiktok_video_count=120, tiktok_is_verified=false, instagram_follower_count=15000, instagram_avg_views_last_10=6400, instagram_media_count=55, metrics_fetched_at set. All three metric_snapshots rows `is_latest=true` (one per scrape_mode). listing.current_version_id wired to v1 without re-triggering the US-010 cascade (the follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id — no versioned-field diff).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` unchanged (seed is pure DML — no DDL).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One cosmetic note (`is_verified=true` on the ig_details row is silently discarded by the denorm trigger since `instagram_is_verified` is not a modeled denorm column — stored in snapshot but harmless; not worth a follow-up migration given append-only rules).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Fixed UUIDs for seeded rows (`11111111-1111-1111-1111-1111111110XX` pattern) let later stories reference the seed without having to query-by-handle first. Later stories that rely on the seed (US-038 discover feed filter, US-055 edit-campaign cascade verification, US-057 override-eligibility approval) should import these IDs as constants rather than re-querying.
  - Safe pattern for seeding an `active` listing without triggering the US-010 cascade: (a) INSERT listing with `status='active'` + `current_version_id=NULL`, (b) INSERT listing_versions v1, (c) UPDATE only `current_version_id` on the listing. The UPDATE fires `trg_bump_listing_version` but `price_cents`/`currency`/`max_submissions` are unchanged and `version_bump_reason` is NULL, so `versioned_changed=false` and no cascade runs. Avoids the alternative of disabling/re-enabling the trigger mid-migration.
  - `denorm_metrics` only mirrors columns that exist on `creator_profiles`. Extra columns on the snapshot (e.g. `is_verified` on `ig_details`) are legal but silently ignored — no Instagram verification denorm column exists per spec §4.7. Don't fabricate values for snapshot columns that don't map anywhere.

## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.

## 2026-04-17 13:00 — US-008
- Applied `us_008_notifications_events` via Supabase MCP: `public.notifications` (6 cols, inbox + unread partial indexes), `public.push_tokens` (6 cols, unique `expo_token`, ios/android check), `public.events` (bigserial PK audit log with composite `(entity, entity_id, created_at desc)` index). RLS enabled on all three; policies deferred to US-009. `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` enforces app-role append-only per spec §4.4 line 1086.
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: three new tables appear with correct shapes (events.id → `number` from bigserial; notifications.kind → `notification_kind` enum; push_tokens.platform → `string` since the spec uses a CHECK not an enum).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417130000_us_008_notifications_events.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables + the 12 pre-existing — all deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One medium observation flagged: append-only enforcement scope for `events` — migration matches the spec literally but service_role retains UPDATE/DELETE grants. Promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-016+ handoff (edge functions writing to events must discipline themselves to INSERT-only; no DB trigger backstop exists).
- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Tables that the spec defines for future-version features (e.g. push_tokens for v1.1) should still be created when they appear in the same §4.7 block as an in-scope table. Partial block creation causes drift that later iterations would have to reconcile.
  - `revoke all on <table> from anon, authenticated` + RLS-enabled + zero policies = full deny for app roles. Useful pattern for anything in `public` that must be service-role-only (events-style audit logs, internal job queues).
  - When the reviewer flags a concern that the spec itself codifies, surface it as a forward-looking Codebase Patterns handoff rather than a migration-time fix — fixing it in the story would drift from the spec, which has a stronger hard-rule in this repo.

## 2026-04-17 18:20 — US-014
- Applied `us_014_auth_signup_lister_fn` via Supabase MCP: `public.auth_signup_lister(p_username, p_email, p_org_name, p_website_url)` — PL/pgSQL RPC returning jsonb `{user_id}` on success or `{error: USERNAME_TAKEN|EMAIL_TAKEN}`. Inserts users + lister_profiles in a single implicit transaction; `get stacked diagnostics constraint_name` maps the unique_violation to the matching 409 code. Revoked from anon/authenticated/public; granted to service_role only.
- Deployed edge function `auth-signup-lister` (v3 after one redeploy for reviewer fix) with `verify_jwt=false` — this is how a lister gets their first JWT. Validates body (non-empty `username/email/org_name`), calls the RPC with the service-role client, mints JWT via `_shared/jwt.ts` (`{sub, role: 'lister', session_id}`), returns `{token, user_id, role}`.
- Provisioned `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` on the project via `supabase secrets set --env-file` (CLI, not MCP — MCP has no secrets tool). Value never echoed.
- Smoke test `scripts/test/auth-signup-lister.ts` covers all four branches: 200 happy path, 409 USERNAME_TAKEN, 400 INVALID_REQUEST (missing org_name), 409 EMAIL_TAKEN (fresh username + recycled email). All four pass end-to-end against the deployed function.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417180000_us_014_auth_signup_lister_fn.sql` (new), `supabase/functions/auth-signup-lister/index.ts` (new), `scripts/test/auth-signup-lister.ts` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — `auth_signup_lister` in Functions block), `.gitignore` (added `supabase/.temp/` — CLI local cache from `secrets set`).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings, only pre-existing INFOs) / mobile-mcp n/a (edge-function-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Medium: (1) added inline comment in RPC exception block documenting that `users_username_key` / `users_email_key` are Postgres's defaults for the inline `unique` declarations in US-004 — so a future constraint rename doesn't silently downgrade 409s to 500s; (2) extended smoke test with a 4th case to actually exercise the EMAIL_TAKEN branch. Nit: simplified `websiteUrl ?? undefined` → `websiteUrl` in the RPC args).
- Spec gap: §5.1 requires per-IP rate limiting via a `signup_attempts` table — that table does not exist yet and is deferred (already noted in the §16 spec-gap pattern for jobs/tables not yet scheduled). Documented inline in both migration and edge function headers so future iterations can wire it up alongside the webhook/session stories.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Atomic multi-insert flow pattern (PL/pgSQL RPC + unique_violation constraint-name mapping) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - Secret-management workflow for edge functions (CLI `supabase secrets set --env-file`, never MCP) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - Smoke-test structure for edge functions (`scripts/test/<fn>.ts` with `bun run`, `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey` headers even when `verify_jwt=false`) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - Default constraint-naming convention (`<table>_<column>_key` for anonymous inline `unique`) promoted to Codebase Patterns — every exception-handler string is coupled to this convention.
  - Reviewer's first review flagged two correctness-fragility issues (constraint-name coupling, untested branch) that looked like nits but would have been real regressions if a downstream migration renamed the constraints — underscores why the mandatory review step matters even on "small" edge-function stories.

content.content:
4:**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
17:| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |
60:### 1.6 Metrics / Apify
64:| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
67:| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
126:(init) ──handle_added[creator]──> linked       (triggers initial Apify scrape)
136:(init, on link or refresh) ──apify_run_started[system]──> refreshing
137:refreshing ──success[system|apify_webhook]──> fresh
148:## 3. Apify Integration
151:- `apify-client` NPM package — [github.com/apify/apify-client-js](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js)
152:- TikTok Scraper actor page — [apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper](https://apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper)
153:- Instagram Scraper actor page — [apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper](https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper)
162:| Instagram | `apify/instagram-scraper` | `apify~instagram-scraper` | Profile details **and** posts/reels — two runs per refresh (different `resultsType`) |
166:- Sync (blocking, returns dataset items): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
167:- Async (returns run, webhook-completed): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
169:In practice we use the `apify-client` JS SDK (`.actor(slug).call()` for sync, `.actor(slug).start()` for async) — it wraps both endpoints.
190:- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).
215:### 3c. Instagram — `apify/instagram-scraper`
276:import { ApifyClient } from 'apify-client';
277:const apify = new ApifyClient({ token: Deno.env.get('APIFY_TOKEN')! });
280:const tiktok = await apify
294:const { items: tiktokItems } = await apify.dataset(tiktok.defaultDatasetId).listItems();
297:const run = await apify
298:  .actor('apify/instagram-scraper')
308:        requestUrl: `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/webhooks-apify`,
317:          'X-Apify-Webhook-Secret': Deno.env.get('APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET')!,
345:- Every actor run costs Apify credits. Published pricing at time of writing: TikTok Scraper ≈ $0.40 / 1k videos (so ~$0.004 per 10-video run), Instagram Scraper ≈ $2.30 / 1k results. Budget conservatively at ~$0.01 per TikTok refresh and ~$0.02 per IG refresh (details + 10 posts = 11 results).
347:- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` returned before any Apify call.
348:[Omitted long matching line]
349:- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
354:- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret; compare via `crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual`-equivalent (constant-time).
355:- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
359:1. Edge function accepts EITHER `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (current) OR `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` (grace). Both env vars are read and compared in constant time; either match succeeds.
360:2. Set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS = <current>`, then generate a new random 32-byte token and set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET = <new>`.
361:3. New Apify runs registered after this change carry the new secret in their `headersTemplate`. In-flight runs (registered with the previous secret) continue to deliver successfully because the edge function still accepts the previous secret.
362:4. After the longest possible run duration (we set `timeoutSecs: 120` + Apify processing ≤ 10 min buffer = **15 minutes**), the janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` will have fail-closed any surviving snapshots. Unset `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` to complete rotation.
385:| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link, one Apify run per row. |
412:-- Which Apify actor run produced this snapshot. TikTok has one mode; IG has two (details + posts).
532:-- 5. social_links   (no OAuth fields — Apify-based)
540:  last_scrape_run_id      text,                      -- Apify run id
565:-- 6. metric_snapshots (historized, one per Apify run)
574:  apify_run_id      text,                  -- dedup across webhook retries
582:  raw_payload       jsonb,                 -- full Apify dataset item(s) for forensics
593:  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
1547:- **Edge Functions** for (a) external Apify calls, (b) multi-table atomic writes, (c) business logic, (d) JWT issuance, (e) webhook receivers.
1570:[Omitted long matching line]
1575:- If it throws (rate limit, 4xx/5xx from Apify) → UPDATE snapshot to `failed` with `error_message`, increment `social_links.fail_count`, return normally. The UI chips the failed mode as "Couldn't fetch" with a retry button.
1577:Signup is idempotent on `(username)` — a duplicate call returns `409 USERNAME_TAKEN` before any Apify dispatch.
1605:Side effects: insert `social_links` + kick initial Apify run (async, webhook-completed).
1632:Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1637:| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |
1639:Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
1643:- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
1645:- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
1650:  -- Apify runs are queued.
1655:### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver
1657:**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
1659:interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
1675:Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).
1678:1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.

content.content:
148	## 3. Apify Integration
149	
150	**Sources:**
151	- `apify-client` NPM package — [github.com/apify/apify-client-js](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js)
152	- TikTok Scraper actor page — [apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper](https://apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper)
153	- Instagram Scraper actor page — [apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper](https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper)
154	
155	### 3a. Actor Catalog (v1)
156	
157	We use **two actors only**, consolidating all metric fetches:
158	
159	| Platform | Actor Slug | REST slug (with `~`) | Purpose |
160	|---|---|---|---|
161	| TikTok | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` | `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` | Profile + last N videos in a single run |
162	| Instagram | `apify/instagram-scraper` | `apify~instagram-scraper` | Profile details **and** posts/reels — two runs per refresh (different `resultsType`) |
163	
164	**Actor endpoints** (both actors expose the same surface):
165	
166	- Sync (blocking, returns dataset items): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
167	- Async (returns run, webhook-completed): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
168	
169	In practice we use the `apify-client` JS SDK (`.actor(slug).call()` for sync, `.actor(slug).start()` for async) — it wraps both endpoints.
170	
171	### 3b. TikTok — `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`
172	
173	**Input (profile scrape):**
174	```json
175	{
176	  "profiles": ["<handle>"],
177	  "resultsPerPage": 10,
178	  "profileScrapeSections": ["videos"],
179	  "profileSorting": "latest",
180	  "excludePinnedPosts": true,
181	  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
182	  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
183	  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
184	}
185	```
186	
187	Notes:
188	- `profileSorting: "latest"` returns newest videos first — required so `avg_views_last_10` is the **recent** trailing window, not all-time popular.
189	- `excludePinnedPosts: true` avoids skewing the average with a single mega-viral pinned post.
190	- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).
191	
192	**Output** — one row per video, with the author profile **denormalized onto every row**. Relevant fields:
193	
194	| Path | Meaning | Maps to |
195	|---|---|---|
196	| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
197	| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
198	| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |
199	| `authorMeta.heart` | total lifetime likes across videos | (stored) |
200	| `authorMeta.video` | total post count | `video_count` |
201	| `authorMeta.verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
202	| `playCount` | **view count for this video** | contributes to `avg_views_last_10` |
203	| `diggCount` | like count for this video | (stored) |
204	| `shareCount`, `commentCount`, `collectCount` | engagement | (stored) |
205	| `videoMeta.duration` | seconds | (stored) |
206	| `createTimeISO`, `webVideoUrl` | timestamps / permalink | (stored) |
207	
208	**Metric derivation (TikTok):**
209	- `follower_count = items[0].authorMeta.fans`
210	- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(items[i].playCount for i in 0..min(10, len(items)))`
211	- `video_count = items[0].authorMeta.video`
212	
213	**One run per refresh.**
214	
215	### 3c. Instagram — `apify/instagram-scraper`
216	
217	The Instagram actor is a **multi-mode scraper**; what it returns depends on `resultsType`. For our use case we need two modes per refresh:
218	
219	**Run 1 — profile details (follower count):**
220	```json
221	{
222	  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
223	  "resultsType": "details",
224	  "resultsLimit": 1,
225	  "addParentData": false
226	}
227	```
228	
229	Output (one row, keyed on username):
230	
231	| Field | Meaning | Maps to |
232	|---|---|---|
233	| `username` | handle | verification |
234	| `followersCount` | follower count | `follower_count` |
235	| `followsCount` | following count | (stored) |
236	| `postsCount` | total posts | `media_count` |
237	| `fullName`, `biography`, `profilePicUrl` | profile chrome | (stored) |
238	| `isBusinessAccount` | is Business/Creator | (stored; informational only — **not required**) |
239	| `verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
240	
241	**Run 2 — recent reels (view counts):**
242	```json
243	{
244	  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
245	  "resultsType": "posts",
246	  "resultsLimit": 10,
247	  "addParentData": false
248	}
249	```
250	
251	`resultsType: "posts"` returns the profile's recent feed (posts + reels interleaved). We filter client-side to `type === "Video"` entries and use their view counts.
252	
253	Output (one row per post) — relevant fields:
254	
255	| Field | Meaning | Notes |
256	|---|---|---|
257	| `type` | `"Image"` \| `"Video"` \| `"Sidecar"` | filter to `"Video"` |
258	| `videoPlayCount` | Instagram's "plays" metric | primary signal |
259	| `videoViewCount` | view metric (sometimes null on older posts) | fallback |
260	| `likesCount`, `commentsCount` | engagement | (stored) |
261	| `videoDuration` | seconds | (stored) |
262	| `caption`, `hashtags`, `mentions`, `timestamp`, `url`, `shortCode` | post meta | (stored) |
263	
264	**Metric derivation (Instagram):**
265	- `follower_count = details[0].followersCount`
266	- `media_count = details[0].postsCount`
267	- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(p.videoPlayCount ?? p.videoViewCount for p in posts where p.type === "Video")` — skip the profile entirely if there are fewer than 1 video posts (set `avg_views_last_10 = null`, surface in UI as "Not enough video posts to compute").
268	
269	**Photo-only creators:** if `posts` returns 0 `type === "Video"` entries across 10 results, we write `instagram_avg_views_last_10 = null` and surface it in the UI as "Not enough video posts to compute." **No fallback run to `resultsType: "reels"`** — the cost doubling (extra ~$0.007/refresh × every photo-only creator × daily cron) wasn't justified in back-of-envelope math vs. just telling the creator to post more reels. Listings that require `min_avg_views_last_n` on Instagram treat `null` as ineligible (fail-closed).
270	
271	**Two runs per IG refresh.** Budget: 1 run = TikTok refresh, 2 runs = IG refresh. No conditional third run.
272	
273	### 3d. Client Pattern
274	
275	```ts
276	import { ApifyClient } from 'apify-client';
277	const apify = new ApifyClient({ token: Deno.env.get('APIFY_TOKEN')! });
278	
279	// --- synchronous short-run (used on first signup — user is waiting) ---
280	const tiktok = await apify
281	  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-scraper')
282	  .call(
283	    {
284	      profiles: [handle],
285	      resultsPerPage: 10,
286	      profileScrapeSections: ['videos'],
287	      profileSorting: 'latest',
288	      excludePinnedPosts: true,
289	      shouldDownloadVideos: false,
290	      shouldDownloadCovers: false,
291	    },
292	    { waitSecs: 60 },
293	  );
294	const { items: tiktokItems } = await apify.dataset(tiktok.defaultDatasetId).listItems();
295	
296	// --- async run with webhook (used for cron refresh and initial IG scrapes) ---
297	const run = await apify
298	  .actor('apify/instagram-scraper')
299	  .start(
300	    {
301	      directUrls: [`https://www.instagram.com/${handle}/`],
302	      resultsType: 'details',
303	      resultsLimit: 1,
304	    },
305	    {
306	      webhooks: [{
307	        eventTypes: ['ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED', 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED', 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT', 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED'],
308	        requestUrl: `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/webhooks-apify`,
309	        payloadTemplate: JSON.stringify({
310	          social_link_id: '<id>',
311	          scrape_mode: 'ig_details',       // or 'ig_posts' | 'tiktok_profile'
312	          run_id: '{{resource.id}}',
313	          status: '{{resource.status}}',
314	          dataset_id: '{{resource.defaultDatasetId}}',
315	        }),
316	        headersTemplate: JSON.stringify({
317	          'X-Apify-Webhook-Secret': Deno.env.get('APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET')!,
318	        }),
319	      }],
320	    },
321	  );
322	```
323	
324	**Signup flow (user waiting):** Use sync `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` for the *first* scrape per handle. If the actor finishes inside 60s, populate the snapshot inline; if it doesn't, the `waitSecs` limit returns a `RUNNING` run and the webhook completes it later. UI shows "Fetching your metrics..." until either returns.
325	
326	**Cron refresh / manual refresh:** Always async (`.start()` with webhook). The refresh endpoint queues work and returns `202 Accepted` immediately.
327	
328	### 3e. Metric Derivation — Summary
329	
330	| Denormalized field on `creator_profiles` | TikTok source | Instagram source |
331	|---|---|---|
332	| `tiktok_follower_count` | `authorMeta.fans` | — |
333	| `tiktok_avg_views_last_10` | `mean(playCount[:10])` | — |
334	| `tiktok_video_count` | `authorMeta.video` | — |
335	| `tiktok_is_verified` | `authorMeta.verified` | — |
336	| `instagram_follower_count` | — | `details[0].followersCount` |
337	| `instagram_avg_views_last_10` | — | `mean(videoPlayCount ?? videoViewCount)` across `type === "Video"` posts |
338	| `instagram_media_count` | — | `details[0].postsCount` |
339	| `instagram_is_verified` | — | `details[0].verified` *(reserved; column add in v1.1)* |
340	
341	Stored fully in `metric_snapshots.raw_payload` (jsonb) for forensics and future backfills — if we later need `diggCount` trends we don't need to re-scrape.
342	
343	### 3f. Cost & Rate Management
344	
345	- Every actor run costs Apify credits. Published pricing at time of writing: TikTok Scraper ≈ $0.40 / 1k videos (so ~$0.004 per 10-video run), Instagram Scraper ≈ $2.30 / 1k results. Budget conservatively at ~$0.01 per TikTok refresh and ~$0.02 per IG refresh (details + 10 posts = 11 results).
346	- **Scale math (back-of-envelope):** 10k creators × daily cron refresh ≈ $40/day TikTok-only, $200/day IG-only, $240/day fully-linked. That's ~$7.2k/month worst case. Sensitivity: cutting daily cron to every 48h halves that; capping free-tier creators to weekly refresh quarters it.
347	- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` returned before any Apify call.
348	- **Daily cron enforcement:** `cron-refresh-metrics` edge function pulls `social_links` ordered by `metrics_fetched_at ASC NULLS FIRST` and keeps a running tally of `estimated_cost_usd`; it stops dispatching new runs once `estimated_cost_usd >= APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET_USD`. Skipped links get picked up first the next day because of the `NULLS FIRST` ordering and oldest-first tie-break. Estimate is static per `scrape_mode` (`tiktok_profile`: $0.005, `ig_details`: $0.005, `ig_posts`: $0.015) — no dynamic pricing lookup needed.
349	- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
350	- Memory: default `memoryMbytes` is fine for both actors (1024 MB); don't override.
351	
352	### 3g. Webhook Security
353	
354	- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret; compare via `crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual`-equivalent (constant-time).
355	- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
356	- Webhook event types registered: `ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED`, `ACTOR.RUN.FAILED`, `ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT`, `ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED`. All four resolve the snapshot (either `fresh` or `failed`).
357	
358	**Secret rotation procedure (dual-secret window):**
359	1. Edge function accepts EITHER `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (current) OR `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` (grace). Both env vars are read and compared in constant time; either match succeeds.
360	2. Set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS = <current>`, then generate a new random 32-byte token and set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET = <new>`.
361	3. New Apify runs registered after this change carry the new secret in their `headersTemplate`. In-flight runs (registered with the previous secret) continue to deliver successfully because the edge function still accepts the previous secret.
362	4. After the longest possible run duration (we set `timeoutSecs: 120` + Apify processing ≤ 10 min buffer = **15 minutes**), the janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` will have fail-closed any surviving snapshots. Unset `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` to complete rotation.
363	
364	### 3h. Rendering Submitted Videos
365	
366	oEmbed for both:
367	- TikTok: `GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>` → `html`, `thumbnail_url`.
368	- Instagram: `GET https://graph.facebook.com/v<version>/instagram_oembed?url=<reel_url>&access_token=<app_token>` → thumbnail + attribution. (Note: this needs an app-level Meta token, NOT a creator token; that's still required for oEmbed but is a one-time app setup, not per-user OAuth.)
369	- Alternative: construct TikTok embed URL `https://www.tiktok.com/embed/v2/<video_id>` directly.
370	
371	---
372	
373	## 4. Supabase Database Schema
374	
375	Applied from `supabase-postgres-best-practices`: RLS enabled on all public tables; FKs indexed; partial indexes for hot filters; `security_invoker` on views; `security definer` functions in private schema.
376	
377	### 4.1 Tables Overview
378	
379	| Table | Purpose |
380	|---|---|
381	| `users` | Unified account row; role-gated. |
382	| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension; denormalized metrics for fast filtering. |
383	| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister fields. |
384	| `social_links` | Creator's linked handles (TikTok and/or IG). No tokens — we don't OAuth. |
385	| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link, one Apify run per row. |
386	| `listings` | Core campaign row; mutable fields. |
387	| `listing_versions` | Immutable versioned snapshots. |
388	| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows. |
389	| `sample_videos` | Sample video URLs per version. |
390	| `applications` | Creator → listing_version. |
391	| `submissions` | Deliverables on an approved application. |
392	| `submission_videos` | N video URLs per submission. |
393	| `notifications` | In-app inbox. |
394	| `push_tokens` | Expo push (v1.1). |
395	| `events` | Append-only audit log. |
396	
397	*(`oauth_states` is gone — no OAuth.)*
398	
399	### 4.2 Enums
400	
401	```sql
402	create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
403	create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
404	create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'archived');
405	create type application_status as enum (
406	  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
407	  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed'
408	);
409	create type submission_status as enum ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected');
410	create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
411	create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
412	-- Which Apify actor run produced this snapshot. TikTok has one mode; IG has two (details + posts).
413	create type scrape_mode as enum ('tiktok_profile', 'ig_details', 'ig_posts');
414	create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
415	create type condition_metric as enum (
416	  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
417	  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
418	  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
419	  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
420	  'post_must_tag_account'
421	);
422	create type notification_kind as enum (
423	  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
424	  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
425	  'listing_version_changed', 'metrics_refresh_failed'
426	);
427	```
428	
429	### 4.3 Denormalization
430	
431	- `creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, `_avg_views_last_10`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from latest `metric_snapshots` via trigger. Enables O(log n) feed filtering.
432	- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, avoids feed join.
433	- `listings.approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count` — trigger-maintained counters.
434	- `applications.listing_version_id` — makes cascade a single UPDATE.
435	- `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` — generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform), btree-indexed for feed filter.
436	
437	### 4.4 RLS Model
438	
439	Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
440	```json
441	{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
442	```
443	RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.
444	
445	### 4.5 Indexes
446	
447	- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
448	- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
449	- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
450	- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.
451	
452	### 4.6 Triggers / Functions
453	
454	All in `app_private` schema (security definer).
455	
456	| Function | Trigger |
457	|---|---|
458	| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
459	| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
460	| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
461	| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |
462	
463	### 4.7 Full Migration SQL
464	
465	```sql
466	-- =========================================================
467	-- 0. Extensions
468	-- =========================================================
469	create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";
470	create extension if not exists "pg_cron";
471	create extension if not exists "citext";
472	
473	create schema if not exists app_private;
474	revoke all on schema app_private from anon, authenticated;
475	
476	-- =========================================================
477	-- 1. Enums  (see §4.2)
478	-- =========================================================
479	-- ... (as above)
480	
481	-- =========================================================
482	-- 2. users
483	-- =========================================================
484	create table public.users (
485	  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
486	  role       user_role not null,
487	  username   citext not null unique,
488	  email      citext unique,              -- nullable for creators
489	  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
490	  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
491	  deleted_at timestamptz
492	);
493	create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;
494	
495	-- =========================================================
496	-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized)
497	-- =========================================================
498	create table public.creator_profiles (
499	  user_id                     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
500	  display_name                text,
501	  bio                         text,
502	  country                     text,
503	  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
504	  tiktok_avg_views_last_10    integer,
505	  tiktok_total_likes          bigint,
506	  tiktok_video_count          integer,
507	  tiktok_is_verified          boolean,
508	  instagram_follower_count    integer,
509	  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer,
510	  instagram_media_count       integer,
511	  metrics_fetched_at          timestamptz,
512	  created_at                  timestamptz not null default now(),
513	  updated_at                  timestamptz not null default now()
514	);
515	create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
516	create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);
517
